title: Xamuel Lennox
content:
{{Top|fa|canon=Lennox}} {{Otheruses|Lennox}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Lennox.jpg]] |name=Xamuel Lennox |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{InsiderCite|96|The Empire's Finest: Who's Who in the Imperial Military}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Dark |skin=Light |cyber= |families=[[Xamuel Lennox's family]]{{Topps|set=2013 Topps Star Wars Galactic Files Series 2|cardname=Captain Lennox—Imperial Officer|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/93283/cid/6569526}} |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Sir, Rebel ships are coming into our sector.''"
"''Good. Our first catch of the day.|Lieutenant Cabbel and Captain Lennox, at the Battle of Hoth|audio=Cabbel-Lennox.flac|{{Film|V}}}} '''Xamuel Lennox''' was a [[Navy/Legends|naval]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] who hailed from a [[Xamuel Lennox's family|line]] of noble military [[Gender/Legends|men]]. Lennox detested the [[Politics/Legends|political]] maneuvering and betrayals among his colleagues in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], but he became adept at deception to safeguard his position as [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Rebel Dream|''Tyrant'']]. He fought in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which he viewed to be threatening to restore a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] that was just as corrupt as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] he served. As a result, Lennox became cynical about the universal nature of deceit. Nevertheless, Captain Lennox [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] Imperial naval [[Imperial Military/Legends|forces]] in the [[Pakuuni sector/Legends|Pakuuni sector]] and led ten [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] in the [[Battle of Turkana/Legends|Battle of Turkana]] in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. The Rebellion proved the effectiveness of its new [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] at the battle, where Captain [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]] was critical of Lennox's tactics that forced the Imperials to retreat. Lennox and the ''Tyrant'' were later attached to [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]], the [[Fleet/Legends|battle group]] of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], which initiated the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] to flush out the Rebellion. Blockading the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] with the ''Tyrant'' at Death Squadron's forefront, Lennox's eagerness to catch fleeing Rebel [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] was quashed when the Rebels' [[v-150 Planet Defender/Legends|ion cannon]] inactivated his Star Destroyer, allowing the Rebels to escape the [[Hoth system/Legends|system]]. After the ''Tyrant'' was repaired, Lennox welcomed an [[Unidentified Imperial soldier (Anoat sector)|Imperial soldier]] and their [[Prisoner/Legends|captive]] Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] aboard his Star Destroyer. The captain lost Madine to a Rebel boarding crew as the ''Tyrant'' was inactivated again by the Rebel [[Planetary ion cannon|ion cannon]]. After recovering the ''Tyrant'', Lennox attempted to recapture Madine from the evacuating Rebel [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] [[Champion (Mon Calamari cruiser)|''Champion'']], but it, too, evaded his attack. [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader then [[Occupation of Bespin|assigned]] Captain Lennox to remove a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Rachi Sitra]], from [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on the [[Gas giant/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. Tasking the [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] who had initially escorted Madine to lead his forces in the [[City/Legends|city]], Lennox successfully minimized interference from Vader's affairs by driving the Jedi away and capturing Rebel sympathizers. The [[4 ABY/Legends|next year]], Lennox and the ''Tyrant'' were present at the decisive [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Rebel victory]] in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]] that ushered the Empire's collapse, and several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, the Rebellion's fledgling [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] captured Lennox and his ship, which they renamed the ''Rebel Dream''. ==Biography== ===Captain of the ''Tyrant''=== ====Pakuuni sector naval command==== {{Quote|Move our Star Destroyers within range and launch all TIE Fighter Squadrons.|Captain Lennox, during the Battle of Turkana|audio=LennoxTurkana-XW.flac|[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]}} Xamuel Lennox was the scion of{{CSWECite|II|250|Lennox, Captain Xamuel}} a proud [[Xamuel Lennox's family|family]] of military [[Gender/Legends|men]] who were [[Officer/Legends|officers]] for several generations. After the [[Declaration of a New Order|fall]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Lennox joined the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|navy]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] with the consideration that the Republic was corrupt and that he would attempt to follow in his forebears' noble footsteps. By [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],The ''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' states that Lennox was [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Rebel Dream|''Tyrant'']] during the [[Battle of Turkana/Legends|Battle of Turkana]]. As the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] used [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] during the battle, it must occur after the Rebels' [[Fresian campaign|campaign]] to obtain the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], which occurred in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] according to ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''. In [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], the Battle of Turkana takes place before the [[Death Star plans delivery]] to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. According to ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', Organa was previously at the [[Battle of Kattada]], which, per [[Empire 5|''Empire'' 5]], was concurrent to the [[Subjugation of Ralltiir]] three [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]—dated to 35:3:8 [[Great ReSynchronization|GrS]] per the reasoning [[Template:Yavindate|here]], and [[Kattada/Legends|Kattada's]] events thus took place in the preceding [[Standard month/Legends|month]], in 35:2. Since the first two months of a GrS year were the last two of the preceding [[Galactic Standard Calendar]] [[Standard year/Legends|year]] per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]], 35:2 was in the year before the year [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]/[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] that was marked by the Battle of Yavin—1 BBY. Therefore, the Battle of Turkana takes place in the same year. during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Lennox was the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Rebel Dream|''Tyrant'']].[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] states that [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron's]] five [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], which according to {{CCG|set=Hoth Limited|cardname=Captain Lennox}} includes Lennox's [[Rebel Dream|''Tyrant'']], were either [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-]] or [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyers]]. Given the inconsistency in the model of Star Destroyer used for the ''Tyrant'' in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], this article only assumes that it is an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]]. [[File:Battle_of_Turkana.png|thumb|left|260px|The Battle of Turkana, in which Imperial forces were led by Captain Lennox]] Lennox was in [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of Imperial [[Navy/Legends|naval]] operations in the [[Pakuuni sector/Legends|Pakuuni sector]] in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], and he received its [[Unidentified Pakuuni sector governor|sector governor's]] order to lead a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of ten [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] to trap and destroy the Rebel [[Warship/Legends|warships]] that were [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Turkana/Legends|Turkana]]; [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] had reported on the Rebel group, which was initially estimated to consist of between four to six [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and between three to eight smaller [[Starship/Legends|vessels]]. Before dropping his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the [[Turkana system/Legends|Turkana system]], Lennox used a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] flyby to verify the Rebels' presence—on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of the ''Tyrant'', Lennox received confirmation from his [[Executive officer/Legends|first officer]], [[Cabbel/Legends|Cabbel]],{{CCG|set=Hoth Limited|cardname=Lieutenant Cabbel}} that their [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]] had located the Rebels over Turkana. Using [[Formation Besh]] in [[Attack Pattern Tartarus]] to keep the surprised Rebels from escaping, Lennox's ''Tyrant'' was the lead ship, with Captain [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]] of the [[Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Emancipator|''Accuser'']] providing primary support as they [[Battle of Turkana/Legends|engaged]] the Rebels' seven [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruisers]] at long range. The two Star Destroyers and the ''[[Flanchard]]'', another of the Destroyers, also launched their [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Fighter wing/Legends|fighter wings]] at Lennox's command. The [[Space warfare/Legends|evading]] Rebels also had two [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigates]], two [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]], four [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 transports]], and a [[Golan Ribbon tanker/Legends|Golan Ribbon tanker]], and their [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers']] [[Engine/Legends|engines]] were attacked by [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] that flew under the protection of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] and [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptors]]. ====Failure over Turkana==== {{Quote|I advised Captain Lennox of the advanced capabilities of the X-wings, but he remained firm that superior numbers would carry the day and did not modify his tactics.|Captain Piett, ''Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide''|}} In under a [[Standard minute/Legends|minute]], Rebel [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] deployed three squadrons of [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] and two squadrons of newly acquired [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]]. Despite Captain Piett's warnings about the [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings']] advanced capabilities, Lennox maintained his tactics of superior TIE fighter numbers. While the defensive screen of TIEs was [[Starfighter combat/Legends|occupied]] by the X-wings, the Y-wings began to destroy the Imperial [[Bomber/Legends|bombers]], prompting Piett to redirect the ''Accuser'' to target the Rebel fighters and allow the bombers to disable one [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser's]] engines. [[File:Captain_Piett.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Captain Piett, who questioned Lennox's tactics at Turkana]] Piett had already been convinced that Lennox's inaction cost an Imperial victory and overrode his fellow captain's orders when the remaining TIEs suffered heavy casualties, moving the Imperial fleet closer to the Rebels into full [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] range as part of [[Formation Aleph]], [[Attack Pattern Abbadon]]. Nevertheless, none of the Rebel capital ships were destroyed, and the Rebel fighters inflicted heavy damage on the Star Destroyers [[Formidable/Legends|''Formidable'']] and the ''[[Ajax]]'' by breaching their bridge [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] with [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]], forcing Captain Lennox to order the Empire's retreat via hyperspace. After regrouping at the world''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' [[Pakuuni/Legends|Pakuuni]], Captain Piett filed a mission report on the battle that was later included in the ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' tactical manual, distributed just before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] [[0 BBY/Legends|nineteen years]] into the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Empire's reign]]. Piett recommended Lennox's removal from command of naval operations in the Pakuuni [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in the report and led the subsequent unsuccessful [[Operation Strike Fear]] counterattack. ===Death Squadron=== ====Scouring for the Rebellion==== [[File:Tyrant-ESB.png|thumb|left|300px|The Imperial Star Destroyer ''Tyrant'', which was under Captain Lennox's command and formed a part of Death Squadron]] By the [[Time/Legends|time]] the ''Tyrant'' joined the [[Battle of the Poln system|battle]] against the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Nuso Esva]] [[0 ABY/Legends|eight months]] after the Battle of Yavin,''[[Choices of One]]'' the starship had garnered a fearsome reputation across the Empire as part of [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]], the personal fleet of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]—it typically consisted of five [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-]] and [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyers]] supporting Vader's [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]], the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], aboard which Piett served as captain under [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Kendal Ozzel]].[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Captain Lennox and [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] Cabbel continued serving as commanders of the ''Tyrant'',{{CCG|set=Hoth Limited|cardname=Captain Lennox}} and, at some point, the former's right hip had been given a [[Myoelectric hip replacement|myoelectric replacement]].{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-11-serving-the-empire|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 11: Serving the Empire}} Cabbel reported to Captain [[Lorth Needa/Legends|Lorth Needa]] aboard{{Topps|set=2015 Topps Star Wars Illustrated: The Empire Strikes Back|cardname=Reporting to Needa|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/112358/cid/7720144}} the Star Destroyer [[Stalker/Legends|''Stalker'']] as part of a temporary detachment of Death Squadron that also included Needa's [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']] in the [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]]. Following the [[Battle of Derra IV]], they retargeted [[Viper probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] in search of the [[Echo Base/Legends|Rebel headquarters]] for the planets [[Allyuen/Legends|Allyuen]], [[Tokmia/Legends|Tokmia]], and [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]][[The Empire Strikes Back: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''The Empire Strikes Back'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]] in the sector. Upon finding the Rebels on Hoth, Death Squadron, supported by four Star Destroyers from [[Juris Sector Forces]],''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' took a series of hyperspace jumps from the [[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector's]] [[Qeimet system/Legends|Qeimet system]] to attack the [[Hoth system/Legends|Hoth system]], where Lennox was joined by Cabbel aboard the ''Tyrant''{{'s}} bridge. ====Blockade of Hoth==== {{Quote|All stations, prepare to fire on my mark.|Captain Lennox, giving the order to target the approaching Rebels|}} Lennox and Cabbel's warship was positioned at the front of Death Squadron's formation as one of six Star Destroyers making up a heavy attack line reinforced by the ''Executor'', maintaining a full complement of seventy-two TIE fighters.{{SWGTCG|set=The Price of Victory|cardname=TIE Hangar}} Despite Admiral Ozzel's mistake of alerting the Rebels to the fleet upon exiting hyperspace close to Hoth, Death Squadron promptly initiated the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] by launching a [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground assault]] on the Rebel base there. Meanwhile, Lennox and the ''Tyrant'' advanced to intercept the Rebels' GR-75 transports and their X-wing escorts as they attempted to evacuate personnel from the surface. [[File:TyrantDamaged-ESB.png|thumb|right|300px|Lennox's Star Destroyer was not prepared for the firing of the Rebels' ion cannon.]] Lennox was beside the [[Viewport/Legends|viewports]] on the ''Tyrant''{{'s}} bridge when Cabbel eagerly informed him that Rebel vessels were approaching their position. Wishing to engage the Rebels even though he had no knowledge of the makeup of the incoming crafts, Lennox announced to his bridge crew that they were about to catch the first quarry of the battle. An [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] relayed that they faced two starfighters and a [[Transport/Legends|transport]]—it was the first Rebel transport, the [[Quantum Storm/Legends|''Quantum Storm'']], escorted by a pair of X-wings. Although Lennox ordered his crew to attack at his command and fixed his [[Eye/Legends|sight]] ahead, the Rebels fired their [[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|planetary ion cannon]] at the ''Tyrant''—an officer reported that [[com-scan]] detected power fluctuations on the planet's surface, but before the captain could request clarification, he identified the flashes of incoming [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion blasts]]—one struck the ''Tyrant''{{'s}} dorsal superstructure while another hit its [[Conning tower/Legends|conning tower]], forcing Lennox onto the deck floor and removing his [[Hat/Legends|cap]]. Lennox's vessel lost its shields and helm control, allowing the Rebel ships to escape the [[Star system/Legends|star system]]. Due to the energy surge, Lennox realized that the Star Destroyer's bridge also lost its [[Computer/Legends|console]] displays and lighting, and the ion blast also deactivated a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and affected Lennox's myoelectric hip replacement, which caused his [[Limb/Legends|foot]] to tap wildly. Despite the chaos, Lennox attempted to stand and told Cabbel to belay his commands for all stations to report, realizing that the ''Tyrant'', albeit veering off course, would not collide with Hoth and that the ''Accuser'' would have to replace his ship's point position. [[File:TyrantDisabled-BTCTG.png|thumb|left|280px|The incapacitated ''Tyrant'' was unable to stop the Rebels escaping the Battle of Hoth.]] The ''Tyrant''{{'s}} bridge further suffered from the loss of [[Artificial gravity generator/Legends|artificial gravity]], forcing Lennox to grasp a handhold while he watched his crew, who were strapped into their stations, attempt to secure a tumbling Cabbel. Lennox concluded from the impact of the [[Planetary ion cannon|ion cannon]] that the weapon was a large emplacement on Hoth, and the captain dismissed his crew's futile status reports as he became aware that the ''Tyrant'' would remain debilitated until after the battle's outcome was clear. ====Hunt for General Madine==== {{Quote|We're blind. We could be boarded by a Rebel ship at any minute.|Captain Lennox, upon the ''Tyrant'' being hit by another ion blast|{{SWGTCG|set=The Price of Victory|scenario=Dark Side scenario campaign}}}} [[File:Captain_Lennox_SWG-TPOV.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Captain Lennox during his attack on the ''Champion'']] Lennox's crew was able to repair the Star Destroyer as the battle raged on. [[Cold assault stormtrooper|Soldiers]] of the Imperial [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] had captured the Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] on Hoth and took him to the ''Tyrant''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]] via a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], intending to transfer him to [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on the planet [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]].{{SWGTCG|set=The Price of Victory|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}} Captain Lennox summoned [[Unidentified Imperial soldier (Anoat sector)|one]] of the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] to report the battle's status on the bridge, but the ''Tyrant'' was hit by another ion blast, prompting Lennox to alert his crew of a possible Rebel boarding action, order his [[Helm officer/Legends|helmsman]] to repair the inoperative systems, and instruct the soldier to secure Madine in the ''Tyrant''{{'s}} brig. Indeed, Rebel [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] took advantage of the ship's ion damage to board it, rescue Madine from the brig, and return to Hoth aboard a stolen [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]] before evacuating with the Rebel cruiser [[Champion (Mon Calamari cruiser)|''Champion'']]. When the ''Champion'' prepared to enter hyperspace for the [[Haven (rendezvous point)|Rebel rendezvous point]], Captain Lennox's ''Tyrant'' attacked the vessel and launched his Star Destroyer's TIEs, offering to call off the attack if the Rebels handed over General Madine. The [[Unidentified Rebel captain|captain]] of the ''Champion'' refused such a deal and launched X-wings and Y-wings that held off Lennox's forces and allowed the Rebels to flee. Ultimately, the Empire either captured or destroyed seventeen of the thirty transports fleeing Echo Base, scattering the rest of the Rebellion. ===Mission to Bespin=== {{Quote|Go to the Bespin system. Remove a Jedi diplomat who undermines the Empire's negotiations on Cloud City. You failed to capture the traitor. Do not fail me again.|Darth Vader, assigning Lennox to Cloud City|}} [[File:Lobot_TCG_by_Barger.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Lennox's operatives captured potential threats to the Empire, such as Lobot of Cloud City (pictured).]] After the Battle of Hoth, [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader sent a communication that Lennox received in his [[Ready room/Legends|ready room]] on the ''Tyrant'' with the Imperial soldier whom he had summoned earlier. Lennox's forces were [[Occupation of Bespin|assigned]] to the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] colony [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] in the nearby [[Bespin system/Legends|Bespin system]], where he would drive away [[Rachi Sitra]], a [[Jedi Diplomat]] who was making trouble for Imperial negotiations with the [[City/Legends|city]]. Vader ended the message warning Lennox not to fail again, and Lennox assigned the Imperial soldier to lead the attack on the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. After two [[Standard day/Legends|days]], the ''Tyrant'' reached the Bespin system, and the Imperial soldier took a number of [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] in a shuttle to Cloud City and forced Sitra to flee from the colony before Darth Vader's arrival with Death Squadron, which had been pursuing [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Rebels]] through the [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Hoth asteroid field]] between Hoth and Bespin. The soldier alerted Lennox of Sitra's departure via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]], and the captain ordered the operative to remove Rebel sympathizers from Cloud City's lower levels, giving the soldier locations to search and names of suspected sympathizers. However, before the soldier could investigate any suspects, Cloud City's [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], advised his people to evacuate. In the ensuing chaos, the soldier captured Calrissian's top [[Aide/Legends|aide]], [[Lobot/Legends|Lobot]], and prevented Rebel commandos from rescuing the Rebel Captain [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] from Vader's forces. When Lennox debriefed the operative aboard the ''Tyrant'', he praised the soldier's removal of the Jedi and their work in keeping the Rebels from interfering with Vader's business, and he further assured that the Empire would provide the soldier with another worthy assignment. ===Endor and beyond=== Lennox retained his post as captain of the ''Tyrant'' and served in Death Squadron at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], where the Rebels destroyed the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] and [[Death/Legends|killed]] Vader and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], fracturing the Empire. Several years later, shortly after the [[Bacta War]] of [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Lennox's ''Tyrant'' was captured by the Rebel Alliance's successor, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. With Lennox [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]], the New Republic renamed his ship ''Rebel Dream'' and made it the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|This is good work. I'm sure the Empire will soon have another assignment worthy of your many talents.|Captain Lennox, complimenting the leader of his operations on Bespin|}} [[File:Lennox_Cabbel.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Lennox and Cabbel on the ''Tyrant'' at the Battle of Hoth]] Xamuel Lennox was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] man with angular features, short [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and brown eyebrows,{{SWGTCG|set=The Price of Victory|cardname=Captain Lennox}} dark eyes, light skin with a mole on his left cheek, and a deep voice. Lennox received years of training and sought to honor his forebears as a noble officer himself. Proving himself to be an adept tactician and capable leader, he commanded the Imperial Star Destroyer ''Tyrant'' and was dedicated to it and its crew; he cared more about his crew's wellbeing than his own political advancement,{{TCG|set=Return of the Jedi|cardname=Captain Lennox (A)|link=https://swtcg.com/Content/Images/Cards/ROTJ/Captain_Lennox_A.webp}} and gave compliments to his subordinates if they succeeded in their assignments. Lennox hated the Empire being mired with corruption, backstabbing, and [[Politics/Legends|political]] maneuvering, unlike the noble values his ancestors had served. However, he was also a cautious man and was wary of being swept away by the war machine of the Empire just as the Republic was made obsolete. Having played his colleagues at their own game by practicing deception as ably as he did warfare, Lennox grew cynical with the belief that the only truth was the universality of deceit. Combined with the Galactic Civil War, Lennox's station took a significant toll on his conscience, becoming disillusioned with the Empire's depravity while also holding the Rebellion in contempt for starting the war, viewing them as immoral and hypocritical—he believed that they were attempting to rebuild a Republic that had been just as corrupt as the Empire they demonized. [[File:Cabbel.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Lieutenant Cabbel, Lennox's first officer]] Despite his command abilities, Lennox's tactics during the Battle of Turkana and his refusal to listen to the advice of Captain Piett led to the Empire's defeat and Piett's subsequent recommendation of Lennox's removal from command of Imperial naval forces in the Pakuuni sector. By the time of the Battle of Hoth, Lennox was familiar with ion cannon emplacements. While some naval recruits could not cope with the constant noise and vibration aboard a Star Destroyer, Lennox missed the thrum while not on a starship, regarding it as the ''Tyrant''{{'s}} [[Heart/Legends|heartbeat]]. Lennox restrained his expressions, keeping his hands clasped behind himself but making eye contact with Cabbel and slightly smiling upon the lieutenant's report of incoming Rebel ships at the Battle of Hoth; he was undaunted by whatever arsenal the Rebels had, confident about his ship and Death Squadron's capabilities, and focused on the approaching spacecraft. The captain was quick to identify the trails of the ion cannon blasts hitting his Star Destroyer and was alert of the resulting mayhem around him but remained calm, analyzing the situation's implications for the Imperial fleet and his vessel while promptly securing himself when [[Artificial gravity/Legends|artificial gravity]] deactivated. Lennox scowled as he concluded that the ''Tyrant'' would remain knocked out of action during the decisive stages of the Battle of Hoth. ==Skills and abilities== The extremely intelligent Lennox had above average strength, but only an average fighting ability. He also had slightly above average speed and diplomacy. Lennox was not [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. ==Equipment== As an Imperial captain, Lennox [[Clothing/Legends|wore]] the standard [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|uniform]] of an Imperial naval command officer, donning a cap and black [[Glove/Legends|gloves]]. He had a single [[Code cylinder/Legends|code cylinder]] beside a [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank insignia plaque]] on his left [[Breast/Legends|chest]] that featured three red bars above three blue bars. At the time of the Hoth campaign, he had received a myoelectric replacement for his right hip. ==Behind the scenes== ===Debut and naming=== {{Quote|I am quite happy with what got through approvals of Xamuel's character. Like Grand Admiral Osvald Teshik and Maarek Stele, I think of him as one of my 'good' Imperials, making the best of ethically impossible circumstances.|Abel G. Peña, who expanded on Xamuel Lennox's backstory|{{EmailCite|email=Wookieepedia:Email/Abel G. Peña|creator=[[Abel G. Peña]]|date=2008-04-28}}}} [[File:Episodev_empirestrikesback.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Lennox first appeared in ''The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization.]] Xamuel Lennox's character and his Star Destroyer, the ''Tyrant'', first appeared misidentified as Captain Needa and his Star Destroyer, the ''Avenger'', in [[Donald F. Glut]]'s[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]] [[April]] [[1980]] [[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|novelization]] of the [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], which was later released in [[May]] that year.''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]'' Lennox appears in the film, where he is portrayed by [[John Dicks]],{{WebCite|url=http://www.markdermul.be/starwars/d.html|text=JediMaster : Mark Dermul's Star Wars Autograph Collection|author=Dermul, Mark|archivedate=20140908051856}} listed among the Imperial character credits as one of the "other officers." Interior Star Destroyer scenes were filmed between [[April 17]]–[[April 30|30]], [[1979]], on [[Elstree Studios]]' Stage 5.''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]'' The [[1996]] ''[[Hoth Limited]]'' set of [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] named the character Lennox, "Xamuel" was made his first name in the article "[[The Empire's Finest: Who's Who in the Imperial Military]]," published in the [[September 4]], [[2007]]{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20070907.html|text=Look Inside ''Star Wars Insider'' #96}} [[Star Wars Insider 96|ninety-sixth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine with writing by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Daniel Wallace]]. "The Empire's Finest," which expanded on Lennox's character, was originally written in [[2002]] for publication alongside Peña and Wallace's "[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]" article in the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine prior to its cancelation. Peña initially intended Lennox's first name to be "[[Wiktionary:corazón#Spanish|Corazon]]," the [[Wikipedia:Spanish language|Spanish]] word for "heart," feeling that it tied into the character's inner conflict regarding the Empire and the Rebellion, but the author changed it to "Xamuel" prior to ''The Empire's Finest''{{'s}} release. Peña decided that borrowing words from another language was an overused method of naming and settled on the [[Wikipedia:Founding Fathers of the United States|United States Founding Father]] [[Wikipedia:Samuel Adams|Samuel Adams]] as an inspiration—although Peña admitted that he was thinking of [[Wikipedia:Boston Beer Company|Samuel Adams beer]] at the time. The author regarded Lennox as one of the "good" Imperials that he had written about and had wanted to revisit the character, whom he described as adapting to situations that were ethically untenable. ===Ambiguity and confusion=== ====Who's who?==== {{Quote|In any case, I did write the BTS part of Lennox's section, and the Piett/Needa gaffe is indeed mine.|Abel G. Peña, referring to his error in addressing the inconsistency with Lennox's line|}} "The Empire's Finest" mentioned that there was initially some confusion among fans about Lennox's only line in ''The Empire Strikes Back'' being erroneously attributed to Firmus Piett; the article explains that although Lennox's face is not fully visible in the scene, his voice was much deeper than Piett's. The article also claims that part of the confusion was due to the film novelization mistakenly giving the line to Piett, but the novel actually misattributed it to Captain Needa. [[File:Star_Wars_X-wing_Cabbel_and_Xamuel_Lennox.png|thumb|left|300px|Captain Lennox (right) in ''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] The introductory scene of the [[1993]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] features artwork that traces Captain Lennox's scene with Lieutenant Cabbel in ''The Empire Strikes Back'', albeit with ungloved hands, and the [[2014]] reference title ''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' confirms that Lennox participated in the events of the game, set before the Battle of Yavin, as captain of the ''Tyrant''. The Imperial soldier serving Lennox mentioned in the article biography is the player character of the Dark Side scenario campaign of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]''{{'s}} [[2010]] set, ''[[The Price of Victory]]''. Unlike his film appearance, Captain Lennox's card in that set features him with a thin brown moustache and lacking his code cylinder. ====Lennox lookalike==== {{Quote|Admiral, our forces have just suffered losses to the Republic at Pallaxides. This is not the time to weaken our fleet.|The commander resembling Lennox, to Admiral Pellaeon|[[Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 5|''Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' 5]]}} [[File:XamuelLennoxLookalike-CrimsonEmpireIII.png|thumb|right|300px|The commander (left) resembling Lennox in ''Crimson Empire'']] A character visually resembling Xamuel Lennox appears in the [[Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 5|fifth issue]] of [[Star Wars: Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost|''Empire Lost'']], the third mini-series of the [[Star Wars: Crimson Empire (series)|''Star Wars: Crimson Empire'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]]. The issue was written by [[Mike Richardson]], illustrated by [[Paul Gulacy]] and [[Michael Bartolo]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[March 7]], [[2012]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54055/star_wars_crimson_empire_iii_-_empire_lost_2011_5|text=Star Wars: Crimson Empire III - Empire Lost (2011) #5}} This article refrains from assuming that the character was Xamuel Lennox. The character had clearly brown-colored eyes and was an [[Imperial Remnant]] commander at a meeting of the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] on the world [[Orinda/Legends|Orinda]] around [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]],{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/crimsonempire3/index.html|text=Back in the Red: Dark Horse Comics Announces Long-Awaited Crimson Empire III Series}} when Admiral [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] convened the group to discuss a [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|treaty]] with the New Republic. After the council received news that the Republic had murdered the Imperial [[Feena D'Asta]]—who was in fact killed by a dissident [[Restored Empire|Imperial faction]]—the commander argued against retaliating against the New Republic, believing that doing so could potentially weaken the Imperial fleet following their prior [[Battle of Pallaxides|defeat]] in the [[Pallaxides system/Legends|Pallaxides system]]. Despite the commander's objection, Admiral Pellaeon and the council chose to strike back at the New Republic. ===Further development=== {{Quote|Call this a "missing piece" from one of the movies—I was always curious what it would be like to be aboard a warship knocked out by an ion cannon, and the fate of the Tyrant in The Empire Strikes Back was the perfect chance to play with that.|Jason Fry, regarding the story on Lennox that he wrote|}} Captain Lennox is among the characters whose name can be randomly selected as an Imperial leader in the [[2001]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''{{'s}} standard game mode,''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' and in its 2002 expansion [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']]. Given that the Imperial leaders' mentions are independent of the game's plots, they are [[Canon|non-canonical]]''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}} [[File:MCQ-ioncannon.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Jason Fry's story focuses on Lennox facing the consequences of the Rebel ion cannon on Hoth (pictured).]] [[Jason Fry]] wrote a story expanding on Captain Lennox's experiences during the events of ''The Empire Strikes Back'' for his April 2012 reference title, ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', but it was cut from publication. The story was instead released on [[StarWars.com]] on [[August 8]], 2014 as part of the eleventh installment of [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut]], which presents various material cut from the book. Titled "A SOLDIER'S STORY: HIT BY AN ION CANNON," the story piqued the interest of ''Warfare'' editor [[Erich Schoeneweiss]], who thought that Fry could further expand the story into a much larger exploration of the impact of the ion cannon strikes on Lennox and his ship. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' {{Gamecameo}} *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone
